name: Shan
notes: Shan (တႆး, Tai Long), a Southwestern Tai (Tai-Kadai) language of Shan State, Myanmar. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: the Shan script (Unicode block Myanmar; a Burmese-derived ABUGIDA) — consonant letters carry an inherent vowel and are modified by dependent vowel signs written above, below, before and after the base, so grapheme segmentation itself needs a syllable-cluster parser, not a linear letter table. A full spec must also model the five/six lexical tones written with tone marks (the 1950s reformed orthography made tone marking systematic) and the final-consonant (killed-consonant) forms. Input is assumed to be the modern reformed Shan orthography in the Myanmar Unicode block.
